feed.title: OpenMedTech Lab
feed.subtitle: A collaborative community developing open-source medical technology to advance global health equity

entry.title: Congratulations to Farjana Taoheed on Receiving an SRI Award

link.title: Congratulations to Farjana Taoheed on Receiving an SRI Award

entry.content:
We are delighted to congratulate Farjana Taoheed on receiving a Spinal Research Institute (SRI) Collaboration Grant Award. This recognition supported Farjana’s participation in the 64th International Spinal Cord Society (ISCoS) Conference, where she presented research on affordable rehabilitation and assistive technologies for people living with spinal cord injury and other neurological conditions.

Farjana’s work focuses on developing low-cost rehabilitative and assistive devices for low-resource settings, helping to improve access to rehabilitation technologies globally. Through the award, she was able to share her research internationally, strengthen collaborations with leading researchers, and contribute to knowledge exchange that will ultimately benefit individuals with disabilities.

This achievement reflects Farjana’s dedication to impactful, patient-centered research and her commitment to advancing rehabilitation and spinal cord injury care worldwide. Congratulations, Farjana, on this well-deserved recognition! 🎉

For more information, please visit SRI
entry.summary: We are delighted to congratulate Farjana Taoheed on receiving a Spinal Research Institute (SRI) Collaboration Grant Award. This recognition supported Farjana’s participation in the 64th International Spinal Cord Society (ISCoS) Conference, where she presented research on affordable rehabilitation and assistive technologies for people living with spinal cord injury and other neurological conditions.

entry.title: Congratulations to Shovan Parvez on Winning the Judges’ Choice Award at the SRI Research Writing Prize

link.title: Congratulations to Shovan Parvez on Winning the Judges’ Choice Award at the SRI Research Writing Prize

entry.content:
We are delighted to congratulate Shovan Parvez on being selected as a Judges’ Choice Award winner in the Spinal Research Institute (SRI) Why Research Matters 2024 Research Writing Prize. This prestigious international competition highlights outstanding spinal cord injury research communicated in a compelling and accessible way for a broader audience.

This achievement reflects Shovan’s commitment to developing practical and affordable solutions that can make a meaningful difference in the lives of people with disabilities, particularly in resource-limited settings. His work demonstrates how innovative engineering, user-centred design, and a strong commitment to accessibility can come together to address real-world challenges and expand opportunities for inclusion.

The recognition is particularly noteworthy given the international scope of the competition, which featured contributions from spinal cord injury researchers across multiple countries and diverse areas of research.

We extend our warmest congratulations to Shovan on this well-deserved achievement. His success is a testament to the impact of collaborative, translational research and serves as an inspiration to fellow researchers, clinicians, and innovators striving to improve the lives of people living with disability.

Congratulations, Shovan, on this outstanding accomplishment and international recognition! 🎉👏

Read the SRI “Why Research Matters 2024” publication here.
entry.summary: We are delighted to congratulate Shovan Parvez on being selected as a Judges’ Choice Award winner in the Spinal Research Institute (SRI) Why Research Matters 2024 Research Writing Prize. This prestigious international competition highlights outstanding spinal cord injury research communicated in a compelling and accessible way for a broader audience.

entry.title: Congratulations to Monzurul on Receiving the SRI Research Writing Award

link.title: Congratulations to Monzurul on Receiving the SRI Research Writing Award

entry.content:
We are delighted to congratulate Monzurul Alam on receiving the Spinal Cord Injury (SCI) Research Writing Prize from the Spinal Research Institute (SRI). His award-winning article, “Reversing Paralysis After Spinal Cord Injury,” was selected for publication in Why Research Matters, a collection highlighting outstanding spinal cord injury research from around the world.

In his article, Monzurul shares groundbreaking research on non-invasive transcutaneous electrical stimulation (tES) and its potential to restore movement and function after spinal cord injury. By communicating complex science in an engaging and accessible way, he helped bring hope and understanding to people living with SCI and the broader community.

This recognition reflects Monzurul’s commitment to advancing innovative rehabilitation technologies and translating research into meaningful outcomes for people with spinal cord injury worldwide. Congratulations, Monzurul, on this well-deserved achievement! 🎉

Read the SRI “Why Research Matters 2020” publication here.
entry.summary: We are delighted to congratulate Monzurul Alam on receiving the Spinal Cord Injury (SCI) Research Writing Prize from the Spinal Research Institute (SRI). His award-winning article, “Reversing Paralysis After Spinal Cord Injury,” was selected for publication in Why Research Matters, a collection highlighting outstanding spinal cord injury research from around the world.
